subject: dwc2: port from socfpga-v4.19.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: USB devices on the DE10-Nano would use vanilla dwc2 instead of socfpga-v4.19 dwc2; both implementations have the same unaligned-IN split transfer bug affecting non-isochronous split-IN transfers with non-4-byte-aligned wMaxPacketSize

license_provenance.author: Synopsys Inc. (2004-2013)
license_provenance.origin_project: Synopsys DesignWare USB2 OTG Controller driver; ported from socfpga-v4.19 (Intel Altera SoC kernel)

notes: Phase 1 APPENDIX: old-branch sweep of MiSTer-v5.14. This commit is NOT on the stock MiSTer-v5.15 branch; it was replaced by version-bump to v5.15.1 which carried vanilla v5.15 dwc2. Stock now uses vanilla 6.18.38 dwc2. The socfpga-v4.19 port adds 24,080 lines but contains NO socfpga-specific tuning or board-specific parameters — it is a generic dwc2 driver backport. KEY FINDING: Both the v4.19 port (this commit) and vanilla 6.18.38 dwc2 contain an identical bug: the unaligned DMA buffer copy (for non-4-byte-aligned wMaxPacketSize split transfers) is only performed for isochronous transfers in dwc2_xfercomp_isoc_split_in(), silently dropping data for all other split-IN transfer types (interrupt, bulk, control). This is documented in carried patch 0028 (Martin Donlon's unaligned-IN fix), which was added to MiSTer-v5.15 and must be carried to 6.18 (already done as patch 0028-dwc2-fix-unaligned-in-split.patch). The v4.19 port is OBSOLETE for the stock build because: (1) it is on v5.14, not stock v5.15; (2) stock now uses vanilla 6.18 which supersedes this port (v6.18 is post-v5.15 upstream evolution); (3) the critical bug fix (patch 0028) is platform-agnostic and applies to vanilla 6.18. USB on DE10-Nano goes through dwc2 — all USB devices depend on this controller. Without patch 0028, devices with non-aligned wMaxPacketSize split-IN transfers would silently lose data, but this is a general dwc2 bug (not MiSTer-specific), not a regression from dropping this port.
